title: Delicate Korean Baby Aubergine Dosirak
description: A winey and shaggy Korean dish featuring baby aubergine, perfect for a date night, made with nimono-simmer and shojin-friendly ingredients, served in a dosirak format.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil to make the nimono-simmer sauce.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the sauce and whisk until well combined.
Cut the baby aubergine in half and add to the sauce, making sure they are fully coated.
Let the aubergine marinate in the sauce for at least 30 minutes.
In a large pan, heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil over medium heat.
Remove the aubergine from the sauce, letting any excess liquid drip off, and add to the pan.
Cook the aubergine for 5 minutes on each side, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the cubed tofu to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the chopped green onions and pitted dates.
Season with sesame seeds and serve in a dosirak container.
